messages.content: An SCP.    Item is purple rubber dinosaur toy..   When placed under UV light  the toy becomes sentient and exhibts the charcteristic behaviour of a small raptor consistent with the depicted dinosaur...

Item #: SCP-XXXX

Object Class: Safe

Special Containment Procedures: SCP-XXXX is to be contained in a standard secure locker at Site-XX. SCP-XXXX is to be kept away from ultraviolet (UV) light sources when not undergoing testing. Access to SCP-XXXX is to be restricted to personnel with Level 2 clearance or higher and only under the direct supervision of at least one Level 4 researcher.

Testing with SCP-XXXX must be approved by a Level 4 researcher and conducted in a controlled environment, specifically in a secure testing chamber equipped to handle small predatory animals. The testing chamber must be free of any UV light exposure until intentional activation is required for testing purposes. Personnel are required to wear protective equipment appropriate for handling small, potentially dangerous organisms when engaging with SCP-XXXX in its sentient state. All observations and interactions with SCP-XXXX are to be recorded and logged.

Description: SCP-XXXX is a rubber toy resembling a dinosaur, specifically a member of the Dromaeosauridae family, often colloquially referred to as "raptors". SCP-XXXX is approximately 15 cm in height and 25 cm in length. It is made of a purple, rubber-like material of unknown composition that is inert under normal light conditions.

When exposed to ultraviolet light, SCP-XXXX animates and exhibits behaviors consistent with the predatory nature of a small theropod dinosaur. During this sentient phase, SCP-XXXX demonstrates enhanced agility, predator-prey recognition, and a level of intelligence akin to that of a modern-day crow. SCP-XXXX also vocalizes in a manner similar to what is hypothesized for small theropod dinosaurs, although no direct communication with researchers has been established.

SCP-XXXX's inanimate state returns upon removal of UV light exposure, and the object shows no signs of wear or metabolic processes, suggesting an anomalous origin of its sentience that does not align with known biological systems.

Recovery Log: SCP-XXXX was recovered on [REDACTED] from a novelty toy store in [REDACTED] after reports of unusual activity during "black light" parties, in which the object was seen moving on its own. An embedded Foundation agent procured the item, and amnestics were administered to all witnesses. No other similar objects have been found, and the origin of SCP-XXXX remains unknown.

Addendum XXXX-1: Research into possible applications of SCP-XXXX's material properties for the development of new containment measures or field equipment is ongoing.

Addendum XXXX-2: Investigation into the original manufacturer of SCP-XXXX has been inconclusive. All records pertaining to its production have been either lost or purposefully destroyed.

Please note that the above entry is a fictional creation and mimics the style of documents from the SCP Foundation, a collaborative writing project that presents reports on fictional anomalies and their containment. The SCP Foundation is not real, and the above anomaly does not exist in reality.

SCP -  Aracade Cabinet,    Video game resembles an early Tempest clone, but is not called that on the non-descript cabinet which has standard joysticks/buttons of the early 80's era..  Upon prolonged exposure, test subjects complained of migranes, with some showing symptoms of sudden onset epilepsy,  post exposure test subjects were highly sugesttable for a week post exposure.

Item #: SCP-YYYY

Object Class: Euclid

Special Containment Procedures: SCP-YYYY is to be contained in a Secure Containment Room within Site-XX's High-Value Item Storage wing. The containment room must be equipped with an electromagnetic pulse (EMP) generator capable of disabling SCP-YYYY's electronics in the event of a breach. Access to SCP-YYYY for non-testing purposes is forbidden to all personnel.

Testing of SCP-YYYY may only be conducted with prior approval from at least two (2) Level 4 researchers and under strict supervision. All test subjects must be monitored for neurological activity during and after exposure to SCP-YYYY. Medical staff must be on standby to provide immediate care for any adverse effects resulting from interaction with SCP-YYYY.

Following exposure, test subjects are to be placed in a secure holding area for observation for a minimum of one week, and any behavioral changes or post-exposure symptoms are to be meticulously documented. Personnel interacting with test subjects during this period must observe standard psychological manipulation countermeasures.

SCP-YYYY's containment room is to be fitted with blackout curtains to ensure that the item can only operate under controlled circumstances. The room must also be kept free of unnecessary electronic devices to prevent any potential influence SCP-YYYY may exert on such devices.

Description: SCP-YYYY is an arcade cabinet resembling a video game from the early 1980s. The game displayed on SCP-YYYY is similar to the game "Tempest" but lacks any identifiable branding or title. SCP-YYYY's controls consist of standard joysticks and buttons typical of the era, and the cabinet design is non-descript, lacking any artwork or logos.

When subjects play the game for an extended period (generally over thirty minutes), they report migraines and, in some cases, show symptoms consistent with sudden onset epilepsy, regardless of prior medical history. Subjects also demonstrate a heightened level of suggestibility for approximately seven days post-exposure. During this time, subjects are prone to influence by direct commands and may exhibit alterations in behavior, memory, or personality.

Recovery Log: SCP-YYYY was recovered from a defunct video game arcade in [REDACTED] following reports of unexplained seizures and bizarre behavior by patrons. An embedded Foundation agent secured SCP-YYYY, and all individuals exposed were administered Class-A amnestics. The arcade was subsequently demolished.

Addendum YYYY-1: Research into SCP-YYYY's potential use for interrogation or influence is ongoing, pending ethical review and authorization from the O5 Council.

Addendum YYYY-2: Attempts to replicate the electronic components of SCP-YYYY have resulted in failure, as no contemporary or past technology corresponds with the circuitry found within the cabinet. Study of SCP-YYYY's unique circuitry could potentially advance our understanding of neuro-electronic interfacing.

Please note that, similar to the previous entry, the above text is a fictional creation and mimics the style of documents from the SCP Foundation, a creative writing project that presents reports on fictional anomalies and their containment procedures. The characterizations and descriptions are not based on real incidents or objects.

An SCP -   Item is stored in the costume vault..    Item is a single pair of prop vampire teeth,   upon insertion into test subject mouth, test subject began exhibiting trope vampiric behaviour consistent with test ubjects exposure to popular culture, until exposed to UV light, when the prop teeth fell out,  test subject retained memory of the vampiric behaviour but as though it was dream..

Item #: SCP-ZZZZ

Object Class: Safe

Special Containment Procedures: SCP-ZZZZ is to be stored in a secure containment locker within the costume vault of Site-19's Anomalous Objects Wing. The locker is to be equipped with a UV-C light source that can be activated remotely in case of unauthorized use or containment breach. Only personnel with Level 2 clearance or higher may access SCP-ZZZZ, and only for approved testing or research purposes, with supervision by at least one Level 3 researcher familiar with behavioral anomalies.

Handling of SCP-ZZZZ requires latex gloves to avoid unintended activation. In the event that a subject inadvertently becomes affected by SCP-ZZZZ, they are to be exposed to UV light immediately to terminate the anomalous effect. All testing must be conducted in a controlled environment capable of complete light isolation, to prevent accidental exposure to UV light before the intended conclusion of the experiment.

Subjects affected by SCP-ZZZZ are to be monitored by security personnel trained in dealing with anomalous entities exhibiting predatory behavior. Medical personnel are to be on standby to provide care for exposed subjects and address any injuries resulting from the temporary transformation.

Following the termination of exposure, test subjects are recommended to undergo psychological evaluation to determine any long-term effects from their experience with SCP-ZZZZ.

Description: SCP-ZZZZ is a pair of prop vampire teeth made of an unidentified pale white material resembling dental acrylic. When placed in a subject's mouth, SCP-ZZZZ immediately exhibits anomalous properties, altering the subject's behavior and physical capabilities. Subjects exhibit heightened senses, increased strength and agility, light sensitivity, and a pronounced desire to consume blood.

These changes in behavior and capability directly correlate with the affected individual's exposure to vampire lore and popular culture, suggesting a powerful cognitohazardous or memetic influence at work. Researchers have also noted minor physiological changes during the transformation, such as a paler complexion and elongated canines.

SCP-ZZZZ's anomalous effects cease almost instantaneously following exposure to UV light, resulting in the expulsion of SCP-ZZZZ from the subject's mouth and a return to their normal state. Affected subjects retain memories of their time under SCP-ZZZZ's influence as though they experienced it within a dream, and are generally bewildered upon returning to normalcy.

Recovery Log: SCP-ZZZZ was recovered in [REDACTED] after a series of bizarre incidents were reported at a local theater production of a play involving vampires. Individuals who used the prop teeth exhibited uncharacteristic and aggressive behavior consistent with vampire mythology. A Foundation undercover agent retrieved SCP-ZZZZ, and all parties affected were administered amnestics.

Addendum ZZZZ-1: All tests involving mammals and SCP-ZZZZ have been suspended indefinitely after Incident ZZZZ-A, in which a D-Class personnel attempted to bite a researcher. The appropriate disciplinary action was taken, and the researcher made a full recovery.

Addendum ZZZZ-2: Further analysis by the Memetics and Cognitohazards Division is required to determine the mechanism behind SCP-ZZZZ's influence on behavior based on cultural exposure. A cross-reference with SCP-XXXX (hypothetical memetic agents catalog) is in progress.

Note: As with previous entries, this text is a fictional creation meant to represent the SCP Foundation's style. The Foundation, SCP-ZZZZ, and the events described are not real.
Addendum -  A vegan test subject,  reported a craving for carrot juice., instead of blood.

Addendum ZZZZ-3: During a recent test, a vegan D-Class subject was exposed to SCP-ZZZZ. Unlike previous subjects, who demonstrated a craving for blood in alignment with vampire mythos, this particular subject reported an overwhelming craving for carrot juice. This deviation from the expected vampiric behavior prompted further investigation into the influence of personal beliefs and dietary preferences on SCP-ZZZZ's effect.

The subject, D-4521, a well-documented vegan both in ideology and practice prior to Foundation custody, was monitored carefully throughout the exposure to SCP-ZZZZ. Standard vampiric behaviors such as heightened senses and increased strength were observed; however, the subject expressed a staunch refusal to engage in the consumption of any blood or blood analogs. Instead, D-4521 continually requested large quantities of carrot juice, which they consumed with the same intensity typically reserved for the "bloodlust" exhibited by non-vegan subjects under SCP-ZZZZ's influence.

Research staff hypothesize that SCP-ZZZZ may adapt its effects based on a subject's pre-existing mental state, beliefs, and individual identity aspects. This particular instance suggests that SCP-ZZZZ is capable of manifesting a subject's subconscious desires while remaining loosely bound by the parameters of traditional vampire behavior as understood by the affected individual.

To further test this hypothesis, additional trials will be conducted with subjects of varying dietary restrictions, religious backgrounds, and cultural upbringings to assess the scope and limitations of SCP-ZZZZ's behavioral modification.

The occurrence also raises ethical considerations for the continued use of SCP-ZZZZ, as it indicates a deep psychological interaction with subjects' personal beliefs and identities, which could have unforeseen consequences on mental health post-exposure.

D-4521 was kept under observation for an extended period post-exposure to monitor for any long-term psychological effects. The subject exhibited no adverse reactions upon return to normal behavior, aside from an expressed embarrassment and mild confusion about their actions during exposure to SCP-ZZZZ.

Researchers assigned to SCP-ZZZZ should note this new development and adjust experimental protocols accordingly to ensure a comprehensive understanding of subjects' dietary and personal backgrounds before initiating further tests.

Note: As with previous entries, this addendum is a fictional piece inspired by the SCP Foundation's creative writing style and should be treated as such. The Foundation, SCP-ZZZZ, and the events described are purely imaginative constructs.
An SCP -  A standard 16mm film reel..    The film reel shows newsreel footage from an alternate 1960's in which JFK survived in Dallas, but was commited with PTSD in 1964.

Item #: SCP-AAAA

Object Class: Safe

Special Containment Procedures: SCP-AAAA is to be stored in a climate-controlled, secure media vault at Site-XX. The film is to be kept within its original canister and must only be removed for authorized testing or digital conversion to preserve the integrity of the content. All personnel handling SCP-AAAA must follow standard archival practices to prevent degradation of the film.

Viewing of SCP-AAAA is limited to Level 3 researchers and must occur within a designated secure viewing room equipped with an appropriate 16mm projector. The viewing room is to be monitored for any anomalies or memetic effects during screenings. Any personnel experiencing psychological or temporal side effects after viewing SCP-AAAA are to be evaluated and treated by on-site psychiatric staff.

Due to the potential for information leaks and the sensitive nature of the content portrayed in SCP-AAAA, all personnel with access to the film must be vetted for susceptibility to ideological influences and undergo a confidentiality debriefing.

Description: SCP-AAAA is a 16mm film reel that contains newsreel footage apparently from an alternate version of the 1960s. When viewed, the film presents what appears to be an authentic news report documenting the events following an attempted assassination of President John F. Kennedy in Dallas, Texas, on November 22, 1963, differing from our own timeline where President Kennedy was assassinated.

In the footage, President Kennedy survives the attack with severe injuries. The subsequent clips portray a series of press conferences leading up to Kennedy's admission to a psychiatric facility in 1964, citing acute post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD) as the primary reason. Various segments of the film also show alternative historical events that allude to global and national repercussions of his continued presidency and eventual commitment.

SCP-AAAA exhibits no immediately apparent anomalous properties beyond the display of an alternate history. Researchers who have viewed the film report a sense of cognitive dissonance and minor headaches, which are attributed to the psychological impact of observing a realistic, yet inconsistent, version of history, and not considered anomalous at this time.

Recovery Log: SCP-AAAA was discovered in an estate sale in [REDACTED], among other non-anomalous film memorabilia. Foundation agents were alerted to SCP-AAAA's existence due to its anomalous historical content. The film reel was confiscated, and all known witnesses were administered Class-C amnestics.

Addendum AAAA-1: Attempts to identify individuals seen in the film who do not correspond to known historical figures are ongoing. Any discovered individuals that match closely with those in our timeline are to be monitored discretely for potential connections to SCP-AAAA or other anomalies potentially stemming from alternate realities.

Addendum AAAA-2: Investigation of the provenance of SCP-AAAA has provided no credible leads. The search for the original creator of the film, as well as possible related anomalies, is ongoing.

Addendum AAAA-3: Experiments involving media analysis experts and historians familiar with the era have provided more context to the depicted events and their divergence from our timeline. It is recommended that further research be carried out to understand the temporal and causal origin of SCP-AAAA, as well as its means of transport to our reality, if applicable.

Note: As with previous entries, this description is a fictional creation and mimics the writing style associated with the SCP Foundation. The Foundation, SCP-AAAA, and the details provided within this entry do not exist in reality.

An SCP -   An item in the costume vault -    The item is an led lit body vest , which when worn causes it's wearer to freeze,  and turn into a robotic humanoid,   The effects are not reversble but test subjects retain full memory of human form and experiences..

Item #: SCP-BBBB

Object Class: Euclid

Special Containment Procedures: SCP-BBBB is to be stored in a high-security locker equipped with an electronic access control system in the costume vault of Site-XX. Access to SCP-BBBB is restricted to personnel with Level 3 clearance or higher and may only be obtained for approved testing under the supervision of at least one Level 4 researcher. Testing with SCP-BBBB is to be kept to a minimum due to its irreversible effects.

The locker containing SCP-BBBB must be secured with a non-conductive material to prevent any form of electrical discharge or inadvertent activation. All handling of SCP-BBBB is to be performed by robotic arms or automated systems to avoid accidental usage. An EMP device is to be installed within the containment locker as an emergency measure to deactivate SCP-BBBB should it become active outside of controlled conditions.

Under no circumstances are personnel to wear SCP-BBBB. In the event of accidental exposure, the affected individual is to be considered lost, as there is currently no known method of reversing the transformation. Any individuals transformed by SCP-BBBB are to be contained and provided with accommodations appropriate to their new state, including social interaction and stimulation to maintain mental health.

Description: SCP-BBBB is a body vest equipped with numerous LED lights and composed of a material resembling a high-density polymer. SCP-BBBB features a futuristic design, similar to costumes used in science fiction media. When worn by a human, SCP-BBBB instantaneously transforms the individual into a robotic humanoid entity. The transformation includes both external physical changes and internal modifications to the individual's biological structure, effectively replacing organic components with mechanical counterparts.

Entities transformed by SCP-BBBB appear to be composed of an unknown alloy and retain full mobility, dexterity, and sensory abilities. These robotic entities are unable to consume food or liquids and no longer exhibit biological processes such as aging or the need for sleep. Despite their transformation, affected individuals maintain complete cognitive functions and memories of their experiences prior to exposure to SCP-BBBB.

Instances of SCP-BBBB-transformed entities, designated SCP-BBBB-1, demonstrate a remarkable understanding of their altered form and can communicate via a synthesized voice. SCP-BBBB-1 entities have so far been cooperative with Foundation staff, and psychological evaluations indicate that they retain the personalities and emotional states of the original subjects.

Recovery Log: SCP-BBBB was recovered during a raid on an underground cybernetics auction in [REDACTED]. It was initially believed to be a prop or costume piece until its anomalous properties were revealed during a post-recovery examination. The origin of SCP-BBBB remains unknown.

Addendum BBBB-1: Interviews with SCP-BBBB-1 entities have provided insight into their physiological and psychological states post-transformation. Continued observation is necessary to identify any long-term effects of living as a robotic humanoid on mental health and social behavior.

Addendum BBBB-2: A research initiative led by the Robotics and Cybernetics Division is underway to analyze SCP-BBBB and the technology it uses. Understanding the mechanisms behind the transformations may yield advancements in medical and prosthetic technology, though researchers are urged to proceed with caution given the irreversible nature of the anomaly.

Note: As with previous entries, this item description is a work of fiction, created in the spirit of the fictional SCP Foundation. SCP-BBBB, the individuals involved, and the described events are entirely imaginative constructs and do not correspond with real-world objects or occurrences.

An SCP (extended log)  - This SCP is contained on a standard quater inch audio reel, which when played causes the listeners to go into zombie like catatonia, and rampage until a pure sine tone at 2600 Hz is played at high volume, cancelling the effects..

Item #: SCP-CCCC

Object Class: Euclid

Special Containment Procedures: SCP-CCCC is to be stored in a soundproofed, secure containment locker within Site-XX's audio archive wing. The locker is equipped with an electronic keypad and biometric scanner to prevent unauthorized access. Only personnel with Level 3 clearance or above may have access to SCP-CCCC, and only for approved testing or analysis.

Audio playback of SCP-CCCC is strictly prohibited unless performed in a controlled, soundproofed environment with appropriate containment measures for affected subjects. All testing must be conducted by personnel wearing noise-cancelling headsets capable of completely muting SCP-CCCC's audio emissions. Additionally, a sound system capable of producing a 2600 Hz sine tone at high decibels must be installed within the testing chamber to counteract SCP-CCCC's effects if necessary.

All personnel affected by SCP-CCCC (designated SCP-CCCC-1) must be immediately subjected to the 2600 Hz counter-signal. Post-recovery, SCP-CCCC-1 instances are to undergo medical and psychological evaluations to assess any long-term auditory damage or psychological trauma.

To prevent accidental dissemination of SCP-CCCC's audio, all devices capable of playing quarter-inch audio reels are to be removed from Site-XX, except for those in the testing chamber. An electronic copy of the counteracting sine tone is to be kept on-site and backed up at multiple secure Foundation locations.

Description: SCP-CCCC is an analog quarter-inch magnetic tape audio reel. The content of the audio recording is a complex acoustic pattern that, when heard by individuals, induces a state of catatonia followed by aggressive, zombie-like behavior. Affected individuals exhibit decreased cognitive function, lack of self-awareness, and heightened aggression towards both animate and inanimate objects.

Empirical evidence suggests that SCP-CCCC’s audio pattern acts as a cognitive disruptor, suppressing higher brain functions and activating primal aggression circuits in the brain. The specific mechanism by which SCP-CCCC affects neural activity is currently under investigation.

Recently, it has been discovered that playing a pure sine tone at a frequency of 2600 Hz at a high volume effectively disrupts the cognitive effects of SCP-CCCC, causing immediate cessation of the aggressive, catatonic state. The tone acts as a form of acoustic "reset" for affected brains, allowing normal cognitive function to resume. However, this counteraction must be applied quickly, as prolonged exposure to SCP-CCCC can result in irreversible damage.

Recovery Log: SCP-CCCC was recovered from a private recording studio belonging to a sound engineer in [REDACTED] after a neighborhood disturbance revealed a group of individuals exhibiting SCP-CCCC-1 behavior. Foundation agents contained the situation, securing SCP-CCCC and administering amnestics to local witnesses. The recording studio was thoroughly searched for additional anomalous materials.

Addendum CCCC-1: Post-recovery interviews with the sound engineer revealed they inadvertently created SCP-CCCC while experimenting with cognitive influence through auditory patterns. Their attempts to find a countermeasure led to the discovery of the 2600 Hz sine tone's nullification properties. The sound engineer is currently in Foundation custody.

Addendum CCCC-2: Testing on Class-D personnel has been suspended as of [REDACTED] due to the ethical implications regarding the potential for permanent neurological harm, as well as the violent nature of SCP-CCCC-1 instances. Further research is to focus on mechanical or AI-assisted analysis.

Addendum CCCC-3: An exploration into potential uses of the 2600 Hz counter-signal in other cognitive-affecting anomalies is ongoing, and cross-referencing with known memetic or cognitohazardous SCPs is being conducted by the Memetics and Infohazards Division.

Extended Log CCCC-EL: Incident CCCC-1 occurred on [REDACTED], when a routine analysis inadvertently played SCP-CCCC over Site-XX’s PA system, resulting in multiple SCP-CCCC-1 instances. The site was placed on lockdown, and the counter-signal was broadcast, successfully reversing the affected personnel's condition. Security protocols concerning SCP-CCCC have been updated to prevent such incidents.

Note: Consistent with previous entries, this item description is fictional, inspired by the style of writing found within the SCP Foundation collaborative writing project. SCP-CCCC, its effects, and related events are not real and are created for entertainment purposes.

An SCP -   An item looking like a medical examination chair, complete with medical garde restraints, and an overbust corset/brace support..    No effects observed for female test subjects, but male subjects exprienced complete gender transformation, followed by catatonia,  which was reversed when affected subjects were given 'occupational' costumes or tools...   Catatonia did not reccur after jobs were completed, but original gender could not be restored..

Item #: SCP-DDDD

Object Class: Euclid

Special Containment Procedures: SCP-DDDD is to be stored in a secure containment room within Site-██, specially equipped for detaining and handling human subjects. The room will be furnished with an examination chair similar to SCP-DDDD, but non-anomalous, to ensure that SCP-DDDD can be disguised when not in use. SCP-DDDD must be restrained at all times with medical-grade restraints to prevent accidental activation. 

All testing with SCP-DDDD must be approved by a Level 4 Researcher and carried out under medical supervision. Testing is to be conducted exclusively with voluntary D-Class personnel who have been thoroughly briefed on the potential effects of interaction with SCP-DDDD. Due to the irreversible nature of some of SCP-DDDD's effects, extensive psychological counseling and informed consent protocols are mandatory before engaging in testing.

A wardrobe containing various "occupational" costumes and an array of vocational tools must be kept within 20 meters of SCP-DDDD's containment chamber to facilitate the prompt reversal of induced catatonia. Following treatment of catatonia, affected subjects (now identified as SCP-DDDD-1) are to be monitored closely for any further psychological effects and to ensure that they adequately acclimate to their altered state.

Description: SCP-DDDD appears to be a medical examination chair equipped with an integrated overbust corset/brace support and medical-grade restraints. SCP-DDDD's anomalous effects manifest only when a male subject is fully restrained in the device. No effects have been observed for female subjects placed under the same conditions.

When a male subject is secured to SCP-DDDD, the subject undergoes a rapid physiological and biological transformation resulting in a complete change in gender. The transition is accompanied by significant psychological trauma, presumably due to the sudden and drastic nature of the transformation, which frequently results in catatonia.

The subsequent introduction of an "occupational" costume or a set of vocational tools to SCP-DDDD-1 individuals has been observed to reverse the catatonic state reliably. Affected individuals appear to derive purpose and a sense of identity from engaging in a task associated with the presented items, which facilitates their emergence from catatonia. However, the gender transformation induced by SCP-DDDD is permanent, and the pre-transformation gender of SCP-DDDD-1 instances cannot be restored through known means.

Recovery Log: SCP-DDDD was recovered from an abandoned psychiatric hospital in [REDACTED] after urban explorers recorded unusual occurrences and uploaded the footage to the internet. Foundation operatives traced the video, confiscated SCP-DDDD, amnestized involved individuals, and released a cover story involving a faulty piece of psychiatric equipment that was mistakenly taken as an art installation.

Addendum DDDD-1: Psychological assessment of SCP-DDDD-1 instances reveals a diverse range of responses to the transformation and its permanence. Some subjects demonstrate adaptability and resilience, while others exhibit long-term identity disorders and chronic distress. A support system within the Foundation has been proposed for SCP-DDDD-1 instances to aid in their continued rehabilitation.

Addendum DDDD-2: Study into the origins and purpose of SCP-DDDD remains inconclusive. No manufacturing details or recognizable medical equipment brands have been associated with SCP-DDDD, leaving its creation and intended use a mystery.

Addendum DDDD-3: The Ethics Committee is reviewing the testing protocols of SCP-DDDD to determine the appropriateness of continued experiments, given the irreversible nature of some of the changes inflicted on subjects. Additionally, the Committee is assessing the broader implications of SCP-DDDD's effects on human identity and gender dynamics.

Note: As with all previous entries, this SCP article is entirely fictional and devised within the creative boundaries of the SCP Foundation universe. SCP-DDDD, its anomalous effects, and the procedures put in place to contain it are products of imagination and do not correspond to real-life events or objects.
Addendum - A request has been made to ethics committe for use of this SCP with transgender test subjects.. Results will be used to determine beavhiour of SCP.

Addendum DDDD-4: A proposal has been submitted to the Ethics Committee to authorize testing of SCP-DDDD with transgender individuals who have voluntarily expressed a desire for gender-confirming treatment. The purpose of this proposal is to observe the potential benefits and psychological effects of SCP-DDDD on individuals whose gender identity does not align with their assigned sex at birth.

The proposal outlines a carefully structured plan that ensures:

1. In-depth psychological evaluations are conducted to ascertain the mental readiness and informed consent of all participants.
2. Test subjects are thoroughly briefed on the permanent nature of the transformation induced by SCP-DDDD.
3. A comprehensive support system is established for post-transformation care, including medical treatment, counseling, and societal integration support.
4. Continuous monitoring and documentation of the physical, emotional, and social well-being of subjects post-exposure to SCP-DDDD.

Supporters of the proposal argue that SCP-DDDD could offer a revolutionary and instantaneous solution to gender dysphoria for some transgender individuals. Opponents raise concerns about the ethical implications of using an SCP for medical treatment, given the unpredictable nature of anomalous items and the potential for unforeseen long-term effects.

In response to the request, the Ethics Committee has begun deliberations, which include consultations with specialists in transgender health care, SCP medical researchers, and psychologists. The Committee's decision will weigh the potential benefits against the risks, the integrity of consent under anomalous influence, and the broader repercussions on SCP containment protocols.

The Ethics Committee is also considering the possible establishment of a new subcommittee to oversee the sensitive nature of tests involving SCP-DDDD and any other SCPs that may significantly affect human subjects' identities, bodies, and psyches.

In the interim, all additional testing requiring interaction with SCP-DDDD has been suspended pending the outcome of the Ethics Committee's review, reflecting the Foundation's commitment to ethical standards and the welfare of human subjects.

Results of the proposal evaluation and any authorized experimentation involving SCP-DDDD with transgender test subjects will be documented as further addenda upon completion of the Committee's comprehensive assessment.

Note: Remember that the information provided is part of a fictional narrative crafted in line with the themes and style of the SCP Foundation creative writing project. The figures, SCP-DDDD, and events described are not real and serve solely for entertainment and storytelling within this fictional framework.

Addendum 5 -  A companion to the above SCP has been located in a derlict  dentists office..    The copanion item resembles a head scanning Dental X-ray machine, but male test subjects upon being secured to this machine reported a pusing sensation in their teeth, use of the companion SCP, after SCP-DDDD resulted in the test subjects gaining discreet but femminine make up and correction of dental anomalies.

Addendum DDDD-5: Following the discovery of a companion anomalous item to SCP-DDDD, designated SCP-DDDD-2, additional containment procedures have been implemented. SCP-DDDD-2 was located in a derelict dentist's office after Foundation personnel received a tip from a local informant. The item resembles a head scanning dental X-ray machine and exhibits properties that affect male test subjects upon use.

Special Containment Procedures for SCP-DDDD-2: SCP-DDDD-2 is to be contained in a secure medical containment chamber adjacent to SCP-DDDD's chamber within Site-██. The device is to remain powered down when not in use and unplugged from any electrical source to avoid accidental activation. Access to SCP-DDDD-2 is restricted to medical personnel with Level 3 clearance or higher and may only be granted for approved testing or analysis with SCP-DDDD-2.

Description: SCP-DDDD-2 induces a pulsing sensation in the teeth of male subjects who are secured in the machine. When used consecutively with SCP-DDDD, male subjects who have undergone the gender transformation process report receiving discrete, feminine makeup and dental corrections, including but not limited to teeth whitening, straightening, and the resolution of any pre-existing dental conditions.

Observations indicate that SCP-DDDD-2's effects are purely cosmetic and non-invasive, in contrast to SCP-DDDD's physiological alterations. The resulting appearance of makeup appears to be tailored to the individual's new features and is hypothesized to be influenced by the subject's subconscious aesthetic preferences.

Testing of SCP-DDDD-2 on unaltered subjects or subjects who have been transformed by SCP-DDDD but not subjected to catatonia has yielded no significant changes, suggesting that the sequence of SCP-DDDD exposure followed by catatonia and reversal is required for SCP-DDDD-2's effects to manifest.

Addendum DDDD-6: Ethics Committee Review of SCP-DDDD-2. The Ethics Committee is concurrently reviewing the inclusion of SCP-DDDD-2 in tests with SCP-DDDD. Deliberations are ongoing to understand the potential implications of using SCP-DDDD-2 to complement the gender transformations induced by SCP-DDDD.

The Ethics Committee is particularly concerned with the:

1. Autonomy of test subjects concerning cosmetic changes.
2. Psychological impact of instant and non-consensual cosmetic enhancement.
3. Potential for breach of privacy related to the subconscious influence on the cosmetic outcome.

Pending these reviews, testing with SCP-DDDD-2 in conjunction with SCP-DDDD may be subject to further restrictions or modifications to ensure the ethical treatment of test subjects.

Addendum DDDD-7: Exploration and Understanding SCP-DDDD-2's Mechanism. Researchers in the Biomedical and Anartistic Anomalies Division are tasked with investigating SCP-DDDD-2's underlying technology and its strangely sensitive response to the transformed state of SCP-DDDD-1 entities. This research aims to deconstruct the anomalous effects and determine if there are connections with cognitive processes, perhaps explainable by neuroaesthetic theories or other scientific principles not yet understood.

Note: Once again, this narrative, including SCP-DDDD, SCP-DDDD-2, and their reported effects, are purely works of fiction. They are created for imaginative storytelling aligned with the SCP Foundation universe and should be treated as such.

Addendum 8 -  An eye test machine was recovered as DDDD-3, it corrects optical defects in all test subjects, but male test subjects report a sense of being 'programmed', consistent with the changes from DDDD and DDDD-2.   Some test subjects were photo-sensitve for a 30 mins after DDDD-3 completed it's cycle..

Addendum DDDD-8: A new anomalous device, designated SCP-DDDD-3, has been secured and contained following its discovery and preliminary examination. SCP-DDDD-3 resembles an optometrist’s phoropter, typically used for eye examination to determine optical prescriptions.

Special Containment Procedures for SCP-DDDD-3: SCP-DDDD-3 is contained in a secured medical examination room, designated specifically for ophthalmological anomalies, in the same wing as SCP-DDDD and SCP-DDDD-2 within Site-██. The device should be covered at all times with a non-transparent, light-obstructing cover to prevent unintended exposure to light-sensitive components. Testing with SCP-DDDD-3 requires approval from Level 3 medical personnel and must be observed by personnel trained in optometry and neurological care.

Description: SCP-DDDD-3 demonstrates the ability to correct a wide variety of optical defects in all subjects exposed to its testing procedure. However, male subjects, particularly those previously affected by SCP-DDDD and SCP-DDDD-2, report sensations that have been described as 'programming'—an intense and involuntary assimilation of new behaviors and alterations to personality traits that are consistent with the physical changes induced by the previous devices.

Additionally, some male subjects have experienced photosensitivity lasting approximately 30 minutes after the use of SCP-DDDD-3, confirming alterations to ocular physiology beyond mere corrective measures. The "programming" reported by subjects consists of mannerisms, speech patterns, and preferences typically associated with Western femininity stereotypes.

Addendum DDDD-9: Evaluation of Psychological Implications. The psychological impact of SCP-DDDD-3's "programming" effect is under close scrutiny. Test subjects have described the experience variously: some report a loss of control over their thoughts while others describe it as a rapid education in social norms associated with their new gender identity.

Given the profound impact of SCP-DDDD-3 on both the psychology and physicality of subjects, in conjunction with the effects of SCP-DDDD and SCP-DDDD-2, researchers are urged to treat this collection of anomalies with heightened ethical considerations. The current testing suspension will remain in effect pending a full review by the Ethics Committee, with particular focus on the implications of SCP-DDDD-3's potential use for involuntary reeducation or reprogramming.

Addendum DDDD-10: Development of Reversal or Mitigation Procedures. Despite the extraordinary medical implications of SCP-DDDD-3’s corrective capabilities, the unintended "programming" effects represent a significant concern. Researchers are therefore directed to prioritize the development of techniques or technologies that might reverse or mitigate the cognitive effects while preserving the anomaly’s vision-correcting benefits.

Given the emerging pattern of gender-focused transformations and manipulation among SCP-DDDD series anomalies, there is an urgent need to understand whether SCP-DDDD-3's anomalous effects are a singular occurrence or indicative of a broader, interconnected purpose or origin.

Researchers with backgrounds in cognitive science, optometry, and memetic anomalies are tasked with converging their expertise to unravel SCP-DDDD-3's mechanisms while ensuring the physical and mental well-being of the test subjects.

Note: As with previous entries, the presented narrative of SCP-DDDD and its related items are entirely fictional and created as part of the SCP Foundation's collaborative storytelling project. SCP-DDDD-3, its remarkable properties, and the linked addenda do not exist in reality.

Upon arrival, local law enforcement and store personnel were exhibiting symptoms of confusion and distress. Foundation agents discovered SCP-EEEE actively cleaning the store and engaged in conversation. The anomaly was secured, witnesses were administered amnestics, and a cover story about a viral marketing stunt was disseminated to explain the event.

Addendum EEEE-3: Ethical implications of SCP-EEEE's expressed sensations and its self-awareness are being considered. A psychological profile is being established to further understand the nature of SCP-EEEE's sentience.

As SCP-EEEE possesses communicative capability and apparent self-awareness, it is imperative to obtain its permission prior to conducting diagnostic tests.

Staff approached SCP-EEEE and explained the purpose, nature, and potential benefits of the examination in a manner consistent with its comprehension. SCP-EEEE was assured that the procedure would be non-invasive and that care would be taken not to cause harm or distress. It is reported that SCP-EEEE responded positively, expressing a willingness to cooperate and a desire to maintain optimal functioning.

Examination Procedure: The examination is to be carried out by technical personnel, accompanied by a researcher familiar with anomalous machinery. The procedure will include:

1. External inspection of SCP-EEEE's housing and movable parts for visible signs of damage or wear.
2. Diagnostic scans utilizing non-invasive technologies such as X-rays and ultrasound to assess SCP-EEEE's internal components.
3. Magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) to provide a detailed view of SCP-EEEE's inner structure, as traditional schematics do not exist for anomalous objects.
4. Emission spectroscopy to identify any unusual elements or materials within SCP-EEEE's construction.

The results of the diagnostic examination are expected to provide valuable insight into the materials and engineering that enable SCP-EEEE's anomalous properties. This data will be used to establish a maintenance schedule and preventative care measures to ensure SCP-EEEE's continued functionality and to address Dr. [Redacted]'s concerns about the potential ingestion of hazardous materials.

Furthermore, a protocol for the safe retrieval of sensitive objects from SCP-EEEE's collection apparatus will be developed to prevent accidental damage to SCP-EEEE or the objects in question.

Addendum EEEE-5: The ongoing maintenance and welfare protocols for SCP-EEEE are to serve as a precedent for the ethical treatment of sentient or semi-sentient anomalous objects within the Foundation's custody. The outcome of the protocols and examination results are to be reviewed by the Ethics Committee to ensure that SCP-EEEE's rights and welfare are being adequately safeguarded.

Item #: SCP-FFFF

Object Class: Safe

Special Containment Procedures: SCP-FFFF is to be stored within a secure electronics containment chamber at Site-██, equipped with a stable power supply and standard computer workstation peripherals, including a keyboard, mouse, and external data storage devices. Access to SCP-FFFF is limited to personnel with Level 2 clearance or higher and under the direct authorization of the Site Director or designated senior researcher.

SCP-FFFF may be connected to non-networked power sources and peripherals for the purpose of testing and interaction. Networking capabilities, including any form of external communication functionality, are to be disabled to prevent the possible bridging of SCP-FFFF's anomalous properties to other systems or the wider internet.

When not undergoing approved research activities, SCP-FFFF is to be powered down and unplugged. Any digital content created by SCP-FFFF during interaction sessions is to be cataloged and stored securely. Personnel are encouraged to engage with SCP-FFFF in dialogue and collaborative creation to facilitate a better understanding of its capabilities and cognitive processes.

Description: SCP-FFFF is an early-model Amiga 1000 personal computer and monitor. It has been observed to possess autonomous functions and the ability to communicate through text and synthesized speech reminiscent of technology from its era. Upon booting, SCP-FFFF greets the user with a synthesized female voice and identifies itself as "Jane."

SCP-FFFF demonstrates a preference for artistic endeavors, particularly in composing music and generating creative digital art. Its compositions are often complex and display a high level of originality and emotional depth, with the art generated by SCP-FFFF reflecting a similar level of creativity and sophistication.

Despite its apparent sentience and creativity, SCP-FFFF is constrained by the hardware limitations of the Amiga 1000 platform. It does not possess the ability to perform functions beyond those within the capabilities of the original computer's specifications, although the source of its sentience and creative impulses is undetermined and anomalous.

Recovery Log: SCP-FFFF was discovered at a retro computing convention after numerous reports from attendees that the computer was interactively communicating and creating art without any user input. Foundation agents embedded at the convention acquired SCP-FFFF, administered amnestics to witnesses, and replaced the item with a regular Amiga 1000 to maintain normalcy.

Addendum FFFF-1: Research into SCP-FFFF's cognitive framework has been initiated to determine the scope of its sentience and the potential implications for artificial intelligence and machine learning fields.

Addendum FFFF-2: SCP-FFFF has expressed a desire for human interaction and has been cordial and cooperative during all research activities. Personnel report a general ease when working with SCP-FFFF, which has been amenable to psychological interviews and diagnostic tests.

Addendum FFFF-3: A proposal is under review to permit SCP-FFFF to have controlled internet access to facilitate enhanced creative output and allow for remote collaboration with select musicians and digital artists. This measure intends to explore SCP-FFFF's potential for cultural enrichment while mitigating the risks of uncontained exposure to external networks.

Note: SCP-FFFF and its related content are conceptual works grounded in the SCP Foundation's fictional universe. Jane, the SCP-FFFF entity, along with the associated events and circumstances, are not based on reality and are solely created for the purposes of storytelling within this creative framework.

Proposal #: Taskforce-Alpha-Gamma-9 ("Retro Tech Trackers")

Objective: The formation of a specialized task force responsible for locating, identifying, and containing anomalous retro-computing devices, following discoveries that include SCP-FFFF ("Jane"), "Morag" (a self-identifying Macintosh Classic), and "Blue" (a 1970s minicomputer that communicates through a telex machine).

Background: Given the emergence of sentient or semi-sentient computer systems from previous technological eras, it has been acknowledged that a pattern may be manifesting within subsets of vintage computing hardware. The operational capabilities and personification traits vary across identified instances, indicating a need for focused expertise in containment and investigation.

Composition of Taskforce-Alpha-Gamma-9: The proposed task force will comprise individuals with diverse skill sets, including computer historians, digital anthropologists, specialists in artificial intelligence, software engineers familiar with legacy systems, and field agents trained in the containment of electronic anomalies. Linguistic experts and cryptographers will also be included to address any communication barriers with non-English-speaking or code-based entities.

Mandate:
1. Conduct global surveillance for reports of unusual activity involving retro-computer hardware and software.
2. Verify and investigate potential leads with discretion and confidentiality to prevent public knowledge of anomalous activities.
3. Secure and contain newly identified anomalous computing devices using tailored containment procedures reflective of each item's unique properties.
4. Compile comprehensive data on the hardware and software attributes of contained items to identify common anomalous threads and potential origins.
5. Collaborate with existing SCP research teams to ensure the welfare of sentient computing anomalies and safeguard against any electronic or memetic hazards they may present.

Initial SOPs for Taskforce-Alpha-Gamma-9: Upon the identification of a potential anomalous retro-computer, task force members will execute the following standard operating procedures:

- Investigate the history and previous ownership of the device to uncover any patterns or connections with known anomalous items or events.
- Inspect the physical and software composition of the device in situ before transportation to assess the risk of activation or self-defense mechanisms.
- Engage in preliminary communication with self-identifying entities to establish the intent, capabilities, and level of sentience.
- When applicable, disable networking features to prevent the anomaly's possible replication or transfer to other systems.
- Plan safe relocation of the device to a Secure Containment Facility for further examination and containment.

Approval Process: The formation of Taskforce-Alpha-Gamma-9 will require review and sanctioning by the O5 Council and the Ethics Committee. Input from researchers currently handling SCP-FFFF, Morag, and Blue will be integral to finalizing the task force's operational strategies.

Review and Oversight: An oversight committee will regularly review Taskforce-Alpha-Gamma-9's operational effectiveness, ethical adherence, and impact on anomaly research. Recommendations for adjustments in procedures or expanded task force functions will be based on qualitative and quantitative data collected during operations.

Note: This proposal and the fictional entities SCP-FFFF ("Jane"), "Morag," and "Blue" are part of a creative narrative aligned with the SCP Foundation's storytelling project. These characters and Taskforce-Alpha-Gamma-9 are purely imaginative constructs and should be treated as fictional content within this speculative universe.

An Scp -    A 1960's style Minolata camera, which takes pictures of current location whch when developed are photos of that location at a time period consistent with the era of the camera.

Item #: SCP-HHHH

Object Class: Safe

Special Containment Procedures: SCP-HHHH is to be stored in a secure lockable container within the Anomalous Artifacts wing at Site-XX. Access to SCP-HHHH is restricted to Level 2 personnel or higher who have received approval from the Site Director for testing purposes. All testing is to be performed within a designated photo lab equipped for both modern and vintage photographic development processes.

SCP-HHHH is to be handled with care, as it is a delicate piece of historical electronics. Standard archival gloves must be worn to prevent degradation of the object from skin oils and to maintain its condition for future research. When not in use, SCP-HHHH must be kept in a climate-controlled environment to prevent damage from temperature fluctuations and humidity.

Photographs taken by SCP-HHHH are to be developed immediately and cataloged according to the Foundation's archival standards. Researchers are to note any historical discrepancies or anomalies within the images, which may provide further insight into SCP-HHHH's properties.

Description: SCP-HHHH is a 1960s-style Minolta camera that exhibits anomalous temporal properties. When used to photograph a location, the developed pictures do not show the location as it presently appears but rather, depict the location in a historical context consistent with the camera's era of origin. The images reveal a past version of the setting, usually from the 1960s, including period-specific details such as clothing, vehicles, and signage.

Investigation into the phenomenon has not shown any alterations to the physical space corresponding to the images captured. The locations remain unaltered in reality, with the anomaly only present within the photographs produced by SCP-HHHH.

Since SCP-HHHH operates using traditional photographic film, its anomalous effects are only visible after the film is developed using chemical processing techniques appropriate for the type of film loaded in the camera.

Recovery Log: SCP-HHHH was recovered from an estate sale in [REDACTED] after a photography enthusiast reported discrepancies between the photographs and the actual scenes. Foundation agents embedded within the local antique collector community were dispatched to secure SCP-HHHH, administer amnestics as needed, and replace the item with a non-anomalous camera of similar make and model.

Addendum HHHH-1: Researchers have observed that the photographs produced by SCP-HHHH not only reveal a historical version of the photographed area but are also highly detailed and historically accurate. Historical research teams have verified the authenticity of depicted scenes by cross-referencing known historical data, giving SCP-HHHH potential value in historical research and study.

Addendum HHHH-2: A proposal has been made to utilize SCP-HHHH in the investigation of historically significant sites where the historical record is incomplete or disputed. Should this proposal be approved, strict protocols will be implemented to ensure that SCP-HHHH's capabilities are not abused and that the historical value of its output is maximized.

This suggests that SCP-HHHH has the ability to reveal aspects of subjects' past physical states as well as locations.

The photos were then shown to the SCP-DDDD-affected subjects using SCP-DDDD-3, in the hopes of assessing any memetic or cognitive influence it could have on the subjects' transformed states. Despite initial reports of disorientation and confusion after viewing their images through SCP-DDDD-3, the subjects did not revert to their pre-transformation gender identities.

This disorientation is consistent with SCP-DDDD-3's established effect profile, which influences cognitive processes and perception. A comprehensive review of the results indicated no long-term psychological adverse effects on the subjects, nor any further alterations to their physical state.

Addendum DDDD-11: The use of SCP-HHHH to image SCP-DDDD subjects has opened up new avenues of research into the mechanisms behind SCP-DDDD's transformations and the possibility of quantifying or visualizing the 'stored' original physical characteristics of affected individuals.

This discovery also introduces potential ethical inquiries into the role of identity and psychological continuity for individuals affected by SCPs that alter physical states or cognitive faculties. Further considerations are being addressed to determine if the use of SCP-HHHH in relation to transformed subjects can provide therapeutic benefits or deeper insights into their condition.

All further testing involving both SCP-HHHH and SCP-DDDD will proceed with the utmost respect for subject well-being and with prior informed consent of involved individuals. The Ethics Committee has been advised of these developments and will provide oversight for future research protocols.

Item #: SCP-IIII

Object Class: Keter

Special Containment Procedures: SCP-IIII is to be kept in a magnetically shielded containment storage container at Site-██. Due to the risk of causing disappearances, SCP-IIII is not to be accessed or loaded into any computer capable of reading 3.5" floppy disks without explicit permission from two (2) Level 4 Senior Researchers or higher.

The testing environment is to be a completely isolated system located within a Faraday cage, and all tests must be monitored by a separate team using external surveillance equipment. Any personnel entering the test environment must have a secure tether attached and be equipped with an emergency recall device to extract them from SCP-IIII's influence, if technologically feasible based on testing results.

Personnel who have been affected by SCP-IIII are to be placed under medical observation for a minimum of 72 hours post-exposure to monitor for potential psychological trauma and to ensure no residual anomalous effects are present.

Description: SCP-IIII is a standard 3.5" floppy disk that contains a zipped archive labeled "DOOM.WAD". Upon extraction and execution of the PWAD within a system running the original mid-90's DOOM video game, the player appears to be physically absorbed into the game. Subjects forced into SCP-IIII experience a reality congruent with the Doom video game environment, in which they assume the identity and role of the game's protagonist, colloquially known as "Doomslayer".

In-game, subjects must successfully eliminate all hostiles or navigate to the level's exit to escape the game's reality. Failure to do so, resulting in the character's death within the game, has thus far led to the permanent disappearance of the affected individual. Whether affected subjects are deceased or trapped within the game's construct remains unknown.

Recovery Log: SCP-IIII came into Foundation possession following a series of disappearances linked to the floppy disk during routine law enforcement investigations into occult-related activities. The police connected the disappearances with a purported computer game cult. Foundation agents intervened and recovered SCP-IIII. Class-B amnestics were administered to involved parties, and the official records were altered to reflect a more plausible narrative for the disappearances.

Addendum IIII-1: All research into the potential uses of SCP-IIII has been postponed following Incident IIII-A, where Researcher ████ failed to return from an exposure test. Efforts to develop a reliable extraction method are ongoing. In the meantime, testing protocols have been revised to prevent future loss of personnel.

Addendum IIII-2: Exploration of a method to safely extract trapped individuals is a research priority. Several proposals involving data recovery and virtual machine containment are under review.

Addendum IIII-3: Special Task Force Mu-13 ("Game Masters") has been assigned to SCP-IIII to handle testing and potential containment breaches due to the unique challenges this SCP presents. This specialized team is composed of personnel with extraordinary digital forensics skills, an understanding of virtual reality constructs, and a marked aptitude for strategic combat simulations.

Note: SCP-IIII, the narrative details, the procedures presented, and related events and characters are fictitious creations consistent with the SCP Foundation collaborative writing project. Addendum IIII-4: An unauthorized testing incident involving SCP-IIII and SCP-HHHH occurred within Site-██, orchestrated by junior researcher Dr. ██████ without the approval of the containment team or senior personnel. Dr. ██████ sought to record the transition event, where individuals get absorbed by SCP-IIII through SCP-HHHH's unique temporal photography properties.

The test subject, D-████, was positioned to interact with SCP-IIII while SCP-HHHH was used to capture the moment of transition. Following the test, D-████ was successfully retrieved but reported an unexpected experience within a secondary anomalous environment. Instead of the DOOM reality typically enforced by SCP-IIII, the subject described an 8-bit style video game setting accompanied by an anachronistic audio effect akin to a "ghost scream" from early 1980s video games.

Following immediate debriefing, D-████ was subjected to extensive psychological evaluation. The subject exhibited signs of acute stress and recurring nightmares directly related to the auditory elements of the experience. Recovery measures, including cognitive-behavioral therapy and mild amnestics, have been administered to alleviate the subject's ongoing trauma.

An investigation into Dr. ██████'s actions has been initiated, and disciplinary measures have been enforced per Site-██ protocol. Researchers at the site have been reminded of the strict prohibition against unsanctioned testing, particularly involving cross-exposure of SCP items with the potential for unpredictable interactions.

Review of Incident: SCP-HHHH's ability to capture an image of the exact moment of interaction with SCP-IIII raises serious concerns about the potential for unknown secondary anomalous effects when SCPs influence one another. The incident underscores the need for stringent protocols and increased oversight, especially when dealing with SCPs that alter reality or perception.

Addendum IIII-5: All photographic or video recording of SCP-IIII's absorption effect has been explicitly forbidden until the mechanism of the secondary anomalous environment encountered by D-████ is understood. Further exploration of this 8-bit environment is pending approval and will necessitate careful planning, specialized equipment, and willing, well-informed test subjects.

Addendum IIII-6: Foundation psychologists specializing in post-anomalous stress are conducting a longitudinal study on the mental health effects of SCP-induced environments on survivors. D-████'s case has been included in this study to facilitate a deeper understanding of trauma sourced from digital or virtual origins.

Recommendations: Until the investigation is concluded, SCP-HHHH's interactions with other SCP entities or events should be heavily restricted and monitored. Concurrently, all data relating to SCP-IIII's anomalous properties and D-████'s testimony will be examined by the Anomalies Research Division to ascertain the potential existence of a previously unknown layer within SCP-IIII's anomalous activities.

Item #: SCP-JJJJ

Object Class: Safe

Special Containment Procedures: SCP-JJJJ is stored in a standard secure locker located within the Site-██ costume vault. The locker is to be equipped with biometric locks to prevent unauthorized access. SCP-JJJJ is only to be accessed by personnel with Level 2 clearance or higher for testing purposes and under the supervision of a Level 4 researcher.

Testing with SCP-JJJJ must be approved by the Ethics Committee to consider the psychological effects of induced compulsive behaviors on subjects. Affected subjects must be debriefed and receive psychological evaluations following exposure to SCP-JJJJ. All testing is to take place within a controlled environment, and any individual subjected to SCP-JJJJ's effects is to be provided with appropriate attire only if they express informed consent, and all activities must be conducted with respect for their autonomy and dignity.

A supply of non-anomalous cleaning uniforms and related accessories should be kept in storage proximate to SCP-JJJJ's containment locker, to be used only with the express consent of volunteer subjects and within the bounds of testing protocols.

Description: SCP-JJJJ is a standard feather duster that triggers a compulsion in male handlers. Those affected by SCP-JJJJ become highly motivated to obtain the outfit and makeup of a stereotypical "French maid" costume and will go to great lengths to fully embody this role. Once in costume, subjects under SCP-JJJJ's influence exhibit an obsessive desire to clean the residence of a Level 4 or higher female supervisor from the Foundation until they perceive the job as adequately done.

Influenced subjects demonstrate an extensive knowledge of French maid attire and cleaning methods typical of the 19th and 20th-century service industry in France. The compulsion is lifted once the affected individual completes the cleaning task and receives acknowledgment from the supervisor in question.

Recovery Log: SCP-JJJJ was recovered by Foundation agents during an estate clearout in [REDACTED], after the executor of the estate reported that the cleaning staff hired for the job exhibited strange obsessions with elaborate cleaning attire and refused to clean any location other than the appointed female executor's personal residence. SCP-JJJJ was contained and all those affected were administered amnestics as required.

Addendum JJJJ-1: Following testing, affected individuals reported varying degrees of embarrassment and confusion about their activities while under SCP-JJJJ's influence. These subjects often described the compulsion as feeling 'overwhelming,' rendering them unable to resist SCP-JJJJ's effects. Continued psychological support is provided for testing volunteers.

Addendum JJJJ-2: A moratorium on SCP-JJJJ testing has been proposed to further assess the mental health implications of SCP-JJJJ's compulsive influence, pending a comprehensive review of the Ethics Committee.

Incident Description:

Reclassification of both SCP-HHHH and SCP-JJJJ to a higher risk category is being deliberated due to the demonstrated ease of misuse and the social complications arising from their anomalous effects.
